metadata.title: release scope us fl manual 2026 05 27 fl ess manual

body:
ACCESS Florida Program Policy Manual INTRODUCTION The ACCESS Florida Program Policy Manual has been designed to be a user-friendly, functional resource for ACCESS staff to use in training and technical assistance. FORMAT Each chapter covers a particular subject. Policies for each program include the following chapter numbers and major subjects:

0200

GENERAL PROGRAM INFORMATION 0400

ADMINISTRATIVE POLICY 0600

APPLICATION PROCESSING 0800

ONGOING CASE PROCESSING 1400

TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS 1600

ASSETS 1800

INCOME 2000

COVERAGE GROUPS 2200

STANDARD FILING UNIT 2400

BUDGETING INCOME 2600

CALCULATION OF BENEFITS 3000

DATA EXCHANGE 3200

BENEFIT ISSUANCE 3400

NOTICES AND FORMS 3600

BENEFIT RECOVERY/FRONT-END FRAUD 4600

GLOSSARY

Each chapter is divided into the following sections:

1. Food Stamps (FS) 2. Temporary Cash Assistance (TCA) 3. Family-Related Medicaid (MFAM) 4. SSI-Related Medicaid (MSSI) and State Funded Programs (SFP) 5. Child In Care (CIC) 6. Refugee Assistance Program (RAP) 1

heading: Page 1

metadata.title: release scope us fl manual 2026 05 27 fl ess manual

body:
ACCESS Florida Program Policy Manual PASSAGE NUMBERING Passage numbers tell the user the following: 1. the chapter number 2. the program area 3. the section number The two digits following the chapter number indicate the program area. These two numbers will appear consistently within each specific program throughout the manual. Any Food Stamp Program can be identified by the “10” following the chapter number. So, 210, 410, 610, 1610 and 3610 are all related to the Food Stamp Program. Examples of these program designators are:

FS

0210 TCA

0220 MFAM 0230 MSSI, SFP 0240 CIC

0250 RAP

0260

After the chapter/program designator there is a period (.) followed by a section number. For example, information on “Asset Limits” is identified in Food Stamps (FS) as 1610.0200; in Temporary Cash Assistance (TCA) as 1620.0200; and so on. This makes it easier to track topics across all applicable programs. These section numbers are in increments of 100s. For example, Food Stamps may include: 1610.0100

1610.0200

1610.0300 When related passages (subsections) follow, these are numbered in order by 1s and 10s: 1610.0101 1610.0102 1610.0103 1610.0110 1610.0111 Sub-subsections are indicated with another point followed by two digits: 1610.0101.01 1610.0101.02 1610.0101.03 By design, new passages can be inserted in the appropriately related program without impacting the existing manual or index. 2

body: ACCESS Florida Program Policy Manual PAGE FORMAT Header Design: A simple format is used throughout the manual. As a quick reference for the user, the program acronym is visible in the upper right corner of each page. Footer Design: The page number is centered at the bottom of the page. Pagination: The page numbering is sequential and is subject to change in updating. 3
